name: Portzilke
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald the milk and let it cool to lukewarm.
2. Add the remaining ingredients (butter, eggs, sugar, salt, raisins, cake yeast, flour) to the cooled milk and mix well.
3. Let the mixture rise until doubled in bulk.
4. Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot, deep fat and fry until golden brown.
NOTE: These raisin fritters were especially made for New Year's Day. The name means "tumbling over," since they turn over by themselves.
VARIATION: Hilda uses 1/4 cup butter, 3 cups raisins, and 1/4 cup water with yeast.
